title: cistus lasianthus subsp. alyssoides
intro: Cistus lasianthus subsp. alyssoides, also known as Rock Rose, is a plant species in the family Cistaceae, characterized by its shrub-like appearance and fragrant, white flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a potential remedy for wounds and skin conditions. Rock Rose has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
